PETITIONER ADVANCE INHERITANCE, INC'S Motion For Order Of Property Not A Dwelling Place For Judgment Debtor is transferred to Dept. 501, the Real Property/Housing Court, to be heard on April 28, 2014 at 9:30 am. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Order To Show Cause Why An Order For Sale Of Dwelling Should Not Be Issued To The Court
Off Calendar Dec-04-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Vann, Esq., 415.621.5645, for Real Party in Interest Horace Wheatley; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Order To Show Cause Why An Order For Sale Of Dwelling Should Not Be Issued To The Court is denied, without prejudice. In its application, Advanced Inheritance has not averred under oath: (1) whether or not the records of the county tax assessor indicate that there is a current homeowner's exemption or disabled veteran's exemption for the dwelling and the persons who claimed any such exemption (CCP 704.760(a)); (2) whether the dwelling is a homestead and the amount of the homestead exemption, if any (CCP 704.760(b)); and (3) the amount of any liens or encumbrances on the dwelling and the name and address of each person having a lien or encumbrance. (CCP 704.760(c).) With the subsequent application, Advanced Inheritance shall submit as evidence an appraisal of the subject property and the calculations for the amount expected to be generated from the sale of Athena Whitley's interest in the property less any liens or encumbrances. Order signed in open Court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Order To Show Cause Why An Order For Sale Of Dwelling Should Not Be Issued To The Court
Off Calendar Oct-23-2012 Continued To Dec-04-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Christopher A. Barnett, Esq., Tel. 408.641.0736, specially appearing for plaintiff;
No other appearance.

The tentative ruling is vacated. Hearing On Order To Show Cause Why An Order For Sale Of Dwelling Should Not Be Issued To The Court is continued to December 4, 2012 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 at the request of the moving party. Plaintiff to give notice of the hearing at least 24 hours in advance. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.
